title: OpenCrane Helm values coherence guards
description: Fail-fast guards for the demo-able shared-tier install (S1 / task_bbafd7e9). Deliberately PERMISSIVE: only the keys below are constrained, every other value passes through untouched. Helm validates the MERGED values on every install/template/lint, so an incoherent overlay (e.g. gatewayProxy enabled with no ingress IP) is rejected before the rollout half-installs.

crds.description: Chart self-sufficiency (#151 item 3): install the ClusterTenant/Tenant/AccessPolicy CRDs via templates/crds/. Default true (self-contained); set false in a fleet-managed topology where the fleet already installed the same CRDs cluster-wide.

install.description: Render templates/crds/*.yaml (each carries helm.sh/resource-policy: keep so helm uninstall never deletes a CRD other silos still depend on).

externalSecrets.description: External Secrets Operator integration. Off by default; when enabled, templates/external-secrets-store.yaml renders the (Cluster)SecretStore + its ServiceAccount for a standalone silo.

provider.description: Only gcp-secret-manager has a provider block implemented today; azure-key-vault/aws-secrets-manager fail fast at render time until their provider block is added.

mainNetworkDefaultDeny.description: Platform-namespace default-deny floor (templates/networkpolicy-main-default-deny.yaml). Off by default; validate plane-to-plane connectivity before enabling in production. Mandatory (enforced by a Helm fail guard) when multiCt.enabled=true.

then.description: When the per-org gateway proxy is enabled, the wildcard A records need an ingress IP and the tenant gateway needs at least one trusted proxy source (use [auto] to derive one) — otherwise every per-org host fails to resolve or every connection fails closed.
When externalSecrets.enabled=true, a provider must be named — otherwise the (Cluster)SecretStore this chart renders has no backend to bind to and fails fast at apply time instead of install time.
